core_angle: Keep shock hook, shorten setup, enter demo faster
hook_line: Holy sh*t, those paws are a mess.
full_voiceover_script: Holy sh*t, those paws are a mess. Quick rinse, less floor cleanup. Snap on the blue hose and wash the mud off fast.
business_purpose: Test whether faster demo handoff improves retention after shock hook
first_principles_reason: Attention is positive but watch time is weak, so reducing the gap between hook and product action should test the retention bottleneck.
expected_user_response: Viewers see product action before they bounce.
risk_note: High-risk language must remain bleeped or character-masked.

core_angle: Keep shock hook, first second must show muddy paws, dirty floor, or rinse action
hook_line: What the h*ll happened to those paws?
full_voiceover_script: What the h*ll happened to those paws? Show the mud, rinse it fast, and keep that dirty water off the floor.
business_purpose: Test whether immediate visual proof makes the crude hook feel relevant
first_principles_reason: A strong hook must be justified by the visual immediately; otherwise attention will not convert into retention.
expected_user_response: Viewers understand the messy scene faster and stay for the product use case.
risk_note: High-risk test variant; do not publish uncensored profanity by default.

core_angle: Casual lowbrow tone without strong profanity
hook_line: Your dog just dragged the whole yard inside.
full_voiceover_script: Your dog just dragged the whole yard inside. Muddy paws, dirty floor, bath time mess. Grab the blue hose and rinse it before the mess spreads.
business_purpose: Compare shock attention against lower-risk casual language
first_principles_reason: If v003 creates attention but hurts trust, casual language may retain relevance while reducing conversion damage.
expected_user_response: Viewers may find it more natural and less jarring.
risk_note: Medium risk; may get less attention but stronger click intent.
